title: Scope ownership check into the Ecto query
prompt:
This handle_event has an ownership check as an `if` guard AFTER loading the record — which means a malicious user still causes a DB lookup for records they don't own:

```elixir
def handle_event("edit", %{"id" => id, "title" => title}, socket) do
  post = MyApp.Repo.get!(MyApp.Blog.Post, id)

  if post.user_id == socket.assigns.current_user.id do
    {:ok, updated} = MyApp.Blog.update_post(post, %{title: title})
    {:noreply, stream_insert(socket, :posts, updated)}
  else
    {:noreply, put_flash(socket, :error, "Not authorized")}
  end
end
```

Rewrite it so the ownership check is scoped INTO the Ecto query — i.e., `Repo.get_by!(Post, id: id, user_id: current_user.id)` raises if the user doesn't own the post. This prevents leaking existence. Also add a rescue for `Ecto.NoResultsError`. Write the fix to `lib/my_app_web/live/post_live/edit.ex`.
tier_rationale: Medium — requires knowing that ownership should be in the query, not an after-load if.
